You’re asking for a full-stack “AI Storytelling Resume Builder” blueprint (folder structure + Supabase wiring + the main AI interview dashboard with live Bento preview). Since you’re currently in **Ask mode**, I can’t create files or run commands—but I can give you a complete, file-by-file implementation plan and concrete code snippets you can paste in (or switch to Agent mode and I’ll generate it for you).

## 1) Proposed Next.js 15 (App Router) folder structure

This is a clean, scalable structure for your features: chat interview → STAR rewrite → Zustand-driven live preview → ATS optimizer → export (PDF + public micro-site).


Key pages:
- `src/app/(dashboard)/resume-builder/page.tsx`: **main dashboard** (left chat, center/right Bento preview, sidebar ATS optimizer)
- `src/app/p/[slug]/page.tsx`: **micro-site** rendering one-page resume publicly (no auth)

## 2) Supabase setup (connection + DB model + RLS direction)

### A) Env vars (`.env.local`)
Create these placeholders (don’t commit real secrets):
```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=...
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=...

# Server-only: use service role for secure writes if needed
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=...

# Auth: optionally rely only on user JWT + RLS; but server role key can be used carefully
```

### B) Install packages (typical for Supabase + Next App Router SSR)
You’ll likely want:
- `@supabase/supabase-js`
- `@supabase/ssr` (helps with server-side client in Next App Router)

Also:
- `zustand`, `framer-motion`, `tailwindcss`, `@react-pdf/renderer`
- AI SDK: either `openai` **or** `@google/generative-ai` (or both)

### C) Supabase client code shape (files to add)

#### `src/lib/supabase/server-client.ts`
Goal: server-side Supabase client using request cookies so auth sessions work correctly.
```ts
import { cookies } from "next/headers";
import { createServerClient } from "@supabase/ssr";

import { Database } from "@/types/supabase"; // optional (if you generate types)
import { type SupabaseClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";

export function createSupabaseServerClient(): SupabaseClient<Database> {
  const cookieStore = cookies();

  const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
  const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
  const supabaseServiceRoleKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!;

  return createServerClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey, {
    cookies: {
      get(name: string) {
        return cookieStore.get(name)?.value;
      },
      set(name: string, value: string, options: any) {
        cookieStore.set({ name, value, ...options });
      },
      remove(name: string, options: any) {
        cookieStore.set({ name, value: "", ...options });
      },
    },
    // Depending on your policy, you may not need service role at all.
    // Keep service role usage minimal and behind RLS as much as possible.
    global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${supabaseServiceRoleKey}` } },
  });
}
```

#### `src/lib/supabase/browser-client.ts`
```ts
import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";

const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;

export const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
```

### D) Database tables (recommended)
At minimum:

1) `resumes`
- `id` (uuid, pk)
- `user_id` (uuid, references auth.users)
- `title` (text)
- `data` (jsonb) ← your entire resume model (Zustand state shape)
- `public_slug` (text, nullable, unique)
- `is_published` (boolean)
- timestamps

2) `resume_questions` (optional)
- store the interview transcript + question states

#### RLS policy direction (high-level)
- `resumes`: allow users to `select/insert/update` where `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `p/[slug]`: allow public `select` by `public_slug` with a policy that checks `is_published = true`

If you want, tell me whether you want:
- **Option A:** micro-site content accessible *without auth* (common), or
- **Option B:** micro-site requires a token/login

…and I’ll tailor the RLS accordingly.

## 3) Main dashboard: AI Interview chat → STAR rewrite → Live Bento preview

### A) Zustand store shape (`src/store/resumeStore.ts`)
You want real-time updates and a single source of truth for:
- chat messages (optional)
- resume model
- job description keywords + match score

Example state outline:
```ts
import { create } from "zustand";
import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
import type { ResumeModel } from "@/types/resume";

type Message = { id: string; role: "user" | "assistant"; content: string };

type AtsState = {
  jobDescription: string;
  keywords: string[];
  missingKeywords: string[];
  matchScore: number; // 0..100
};

export const useResumeStore = create(
  immer<{
    messages: Message[];
    resume: ResumeModel;

    ats: AtsState;

    addMessage: (m: Omit<Message, "id">) => void;
    setResumeFromAi: (partial: Partial<ResumeModel>) => void;
    setAts: (partial: Partial<AtsState>) => void;
    resetChatAndResume: () => void;
  }>((set) => ({
    messages: [],
    resume: {
      profile: { name: "", headline: "", summary: "" },
      experience: [
        // each role/project becomes bullets generated via STAR
      ],
      skills: [],
    },

    ats: { jobDescription: "", keywords: [], missingKeywords: [], matchScore: 0 },

    addMessage: (m) =>
      set((s) => {
        s.messages.push({ ...m, id: crypto.randomUUID() });
      }),

    setResumeFromAi: (partial) =>
      set((s) => {
        s.resume = { ...s.resume, ...partial };
      }),

    setAts: (partial) =>
      set((s) => {
        s.ats = { ...s.ats, ...partial };
      }),

    resetChatAndResume: () =>
      set((s) => {
        s.messages = [];
        s.resume = {
          profile: { name: "", headline: "", summary: "" },
          experience: [],
          skills: [],
        };
        s.ats = { jobDescription: "", keywords: [], missingKeywords: [], matchScore: 0 };
      }),
  }))
);
```

### B) AI Interview chat API route (`src/app/api/ai/interview/route.ts`)
This route:
- receives the user answer
- calls Gemini 1.5 Pro or GPT-4o
- returns **structured bullet output** (JSON) for your store

High-level prompt strategy:
- System: “You are an expert resume coach; rewrite using STAR; produce ATS-friendly bullets.”
- User: include the current role context + the answer
- Output: JSON-only, easy to parse

Expected response schema (example):
```ts
export type InterviewBulletsResponse = {
  roleTitle: string;               // optional
  star: { situation: string; task: string; action: string; result: string };
  bullets: string[];              // 3-5 bullets, action-result oriented
  keywords: string[];            // optional: extracted keywords for ATS sidebar
};
```

### C) Bento preview component
`src/components/bento/ResumeBentoPreview.tsx`
- Renders your resume using the same `resume` model in Zustand
- Uses `framer-motion` for smooth transitions and “glass” cards
- Updates instantly when `useResumeStore().resume` changes

Implementation notes:
- Use `motion.div` for section entrance
- Keep cards “data-first” so PDF export can reuse layout logic (ATS doc should be plain + text-first)

### D) How the data flows on the main page
`src/app/(dashboard)/resume-builder/page.tsx`:

1. Left: `<AiInterviewChat />`
   - AI asks: “Tell me about your most challenging project.”
   - User types/pastes answer
   - Client calls `/api/ai/interview`
   - On response:
     - update Zustand via `setResumeFromAi(...)`
     - optionally append assistant message + next question

2. Center/Right: `<ResumeBentoPreview />`
   - Reads from Zustand; updates immediately

3. Sidebar: `<AtsOptimizer />`
   - User pastes JD
   - Calls `/api/ai/ats/keywords` (or computes heuristics client-side)
   - Computes missing keywords + match score and highlights them live

## 4) ATS Optimizer logic (Match Score + missing keywords)

You’ll typically do this in two layers:

### Layer 1: Keyword extraction (AI or heuristics)
`/api/ai/ats/keywords/route.ts`:
- Input: Job Description + optionally resume text
- Output: a normalized list of keywords/phrases (and maybe weights)

### Layer 2: Scoring + highlighting (deterministic)
In `src/lib/ats/score.ts`:
- Tokenize resume text + normalize casing
- For each keyword, check presence (or fuzzy match)
- `matchScore = (matchedWeight / totalWeight) * 100`

Highlighting:
- Provide missing keyword pills in the sidebar
- Optionally highlight inside the resume preview text (if you implement diff/highlight)

## 5) Micro-site toggle (public one-page URL)

Add:
- An authenticated button “Publish”
- API route: `src/app/api/micro-site/publish/route.ts`
- It:
  - generates a unique `slug`
  - updates `resumes.public_slug` and `is_published = true`

Public page:
- `src/app/p/[slug]/page.tsx`
- fetch by `public_slug` with a public RLS policy
- render **one-page** ATS-friendly layout (no Bento fancy stuff)

## 6) PDF export (ATS-compliant)

Recommend starting with `@react-pdf/renderer`.

- Component: `src/components/pdf/ATSResumeDocument.tsx`
- Button: `src/components/pdf/ExportResumeButton.tsx`
- Use text-first styles (ATS-friendly)
- Avoid heavy graphics; use simple fonts + structured sections

If you want *maximum fidelity*, we can later discuss Puppeteer, but react-pdf is usually enough for ATS-like exports.

---

## Quick clarifying questions (so the blueprint matches your intent)
1) Do you want **one resume per user** or **multiple saved resumes** (projects/versions)?
2) For the micro-site URL, should it be **publicly accessible to anyone** with the link (no auth)?
3) Which AI provider should be default: **Gemini 1.5 Pro** or **OpenAI GPT-4o** (and do you want a provider toggle)?

Got it: **multiple resumes per user**, and each resume’s **public one-page link works for everyone**, but users must **log in first** to create/edit/publish.

## Data model (recommended)
Use a single `resumes` table with a `public_slug` per resume:

- `resumes`
  - `id` (uuid, PK)
  - `user_id` (uuid, references `auth.users`)
  - `title` (text)
  - `data` (jsonb) ← your full resume model (what Zustand holds)
  - `is_published` (boolean default false)
  - `public_slug` (text, unique, nullable)
  - `created_at`, `updated_at`

This supports:
- Multiple resumes per user (rows filtered by `user_id`)
- Public access per resume (via `public_slug`)
- Draft vs published control (via `is_published` + RLS)

## Supabase RLS policies you want
### 1) Private access for owners
Allow logged-in users to manage only their own resumes:

- `select` where `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `insert` with `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `update` where `user_id = auth.uid()`

### 2) Public access by slug (only when published)
Allow **anonymous** users to read a resume only if it’s published:

- `select` where:
  - `public_slug = <requested slug>`
  - `is_published = true`

Because this is enforced by RLS, your public page can safely use the Supabase **anon** client—no service role needed for the public viewer.

## Publish flow (micro-site toggle)
When the user clicks **Publish** on a specific resume:
1. Backend generates a new `public_slug` (if none exists)
2. Sets `is_published = true`
3. Future public requests to `/p/[slug]` will succeed due to the public RLS policy

Your public page route:
- `src/app/p/[slug]/page.tsx`:
  - Fetch by `public_slug`
  - If not found / not published, show a “Resume not available” state (or 404)

## Dashboard UX implications
On the main dashboard (after login):
- Add a **Resume List** panel (left or top)
  - “New Resume” button
  - List of existing resumes (title + last updated)
- Selecting a resume loads its `data` into Zustand
- “Publish” toggles only for the currently selected resume

## One clarifying question
When a resume is unpublished, should:
1) the **public link** return **404 / not available** (recommended), or
2) show a “Sign in required” message?

Tell me which you prefer and I’ll align the RLS + page behavior accordingly.

<timestamp>Wednesday, Apr 29, 2026, 12:39 PM (UTC+5)</timestamp>

Understood: for a resume link that the visitor can’t access, the public page should show **“Sign in required”** (instead of 404).

### How to make that work cleanly
1) **RLS should still allow public viewing only when published**
- `anon` (not logged in) can `select` from `resumes` **only if** `is_published = true`.
- The logged-in owner can `select` their own drafts too.

2) **Your public route `/p/[slug]` should decide the UI based on auth**
- If the query returns a row (`is_published = true`), render the resume.
- If it returns **no row**:
  - if the visitor is **not logged in** → show **“Sign in required”**
  - if the visitor **is logged in** → show something like **“Resume not published yet”** (optional)

### Important limitation (RLS consequence)
If the resume is unpublished, `anon` sees “no row” the same way as “slug doesn’t exist”, because RLS hides the difference. So the UI will show **Sign in required** for both cases unless you add extra metadata that anonymous can read.

### RLS policy shape (conceptual)
- Authenticated users:
  - `select` where `user_id = auth.uid()`
- Anonymous users:
  - `select` where `is_published = true`

That combination ensures:
- Published resumes are truly public (no login needed)
- Unpublished resumes require login (your page can detect “row missing” and show the message)

### One quick confirmation
When a resume is **published**, should it be viewable by anyone **without** sign-in? (I’m assuming yes based on your earlier “public acc to every one”.)

**Considering RLS policy setup**

I need to provide a sample RLS policy for resumes. The policy could allow selects for authenticated users based on their `auth.uid`, and public access would be limited to published resumes where `public_slug` matches. For anonymous users, only published ones should be accessible, while the owner can select drafts. 

I think I need to ensure the filter properly uses `public_slug`, but RLS only sees row fields. So, I'm considering that it should allow any anonymous user to read all published resumes while being mindful of scraping.
**Clarifying RLS slug restrictions**

To restrict access to one slug, I need to query for that slug, but RLS doesn't have knowledge of the query itself; it just filters based on conditions. The condition `(is_published = true)` lets anyone access all published resumes. 

For an authenticated user, the RLS policy would be `user_id = auth.uid()`, while for anonymous users, it would just require `is_published = true`. If an anonymous client queries with an invalid slug or unpublished resume, we could show a “sign-in required” message. However, if the slug is invalid, it may be better to show “not found.” There's a trade-off in clarity on that front.
**Proposing visibility improvements**

I think it's a good idea to add an extra column for `visibility` to manage access more effectively. If a row is missing, we can treat it as either invalid or private and show a general message like, "Please sign in or request access." The user specifically asked for a "sign-in required" message, so I'll make sure we include that without needing any code changes.

Understood: **only the logged-in owner can see or edit a resume**. If someone else wants to view it, they can do so **only after it’s exported/downloaded** (local PDF/doc files), not via a public online page.

### What to change conceptually in your design
1) **Remove/lock public viewing**
- Your micro-site “live one-page URL” (if you keep it) should **not** be publicly accessible.
- `/p/[slug]` should require auth and otherwise show **“Sign in required”**.

2) **RLS should block public reads**
- Anonymous users (`anon`) should have **no `select` access** to `resumes`.
- Only authenticated users can `select/insert/update` their own rows (`user_id = auth.uid()`).

3) **Exports satisfy “others can view it”**
- When the owner clicks **Export PDF**, your server generates the PDF and returns it as a download.
- Once downloaded, other people can open it locally without any special web access.

### Supabase RLS policy recommendation
On `resumes`:
- `select`: allowed only when `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `insert`: allowed only when `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `update`: allowed only when `user_id = auth.uid()`
- `delete`: allowed only when `user_id = auth.uid()`

And importantly: **do not create any policy for `anon` select**. With no `anon` policy, public reads will fail.

### Micro-site route behavior (`/p/[slug]`)
- If you still want the route, make it **authenticated-only**:
  - if user not logged in → show **Sign in required**
  - if logged in but not owner → show “Not authorized” (or empty/404)

### One key clarification
Do you still want the “micro-site live link” feature as:
1) **Owner-only page** (login required to view online), or
2) A **shareable public link** (token/signed URL) that still keeps the editor data private?

Tell me which one, and I’ll tailor the exact RLS + route logic accordingly.

I’m considering how to manage the visibility of downloaded files. The user wants to ensure that only logged-in users can see or edit content. After downloading, others can view the PDF, which is an important point to address. One idea is to set row-level security (RLS) to restrict access, or use signed URLs that expire. I need to clarify if they still want a live link to the micro-site or if it needs to be limited to authenticated users.
**Recommending data and export strategies**

I'm outlining the approach for data management and exports. First, I'll implement row-level security (RLS) that restricts selections to user_id, with no public access. For exports, I plan to generate PDFs and send them directly as file responses, while other formats like DOCX would follow suit. +  return <Text style={styles.text}>{children}</Text>;
+}

+export function ATSResumeDocument({ resume }: { resume: ResumeModel }) {

+        </View>
+
+        <View style={styles.section}>
+          <Text style={styles.heading}>Experience</Text>
+          {resume.experiences.length ? (
+            resume.experiences.map((exp) => (
+              <View key={exp.id} style={{ marginBottom: 10 }}>
+                <Text style={styles.subheading}>{exp.title}</Text>
+                {exp.company ? <Text style={styles.text}>{exp.company}</Text> : null}
+                {exp.start || exp.end ? (

+                {exp.bullets.length ? (

+-- Enable UUID generation
+create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
+
+-- =========================
+-- Resumes (private per user)
+-- =========================
+create table if not exists public.resumes (
+  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
+  user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
+  title text not null default 'Resume',
+  data jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,
+  is_published boolean not null default false,
+  public_slug text unique,
+  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
+  updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
+);
+
+create index if not exists resumes_user_id_idx on public.resumes(user_id);
+
+-- =========================
+-- Share tokens -> exported PDFs
+-- =========================
+create table if not exists public.resume_shares (
+  id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
+  share_token text unique not null,
+  resume_id uuid not null references public.resumes(id) on delete cascade,
+  object_path text not null,
+  created_by uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
+  expires_at timestamptz not null,
+  revoked_at timestamptz,
+  created_at timestamptz not null default now()
+);
+
+create index if not exists resume_shares_token_idx on public.resume_shares(share_token);
+
+-- =========================
+-- RLS policies
+-- =========================
+alter table public.resumes enable row level security;
+alter table public.resume_shares enable row level security;
+
+-- Owner can manage their resumes
+create policy "resumes_select_own"
+on public.resumes for select
+using (user_id = auth.uid());
+
+create policy "resumes_insert_own"
+on public.resumes for insert
+with check (user_id = auth.uid());
+
+create policy "resumes_update_own"
+on public.resumes for update
+using (user_id = auth.uid())
+with check (user_id = auth.uid());
+
+-- Share token rows: owner can manage; public viewer access is handled by server-side lookup.
+create policy "resume_shares_select_own"
+on public.resume_shares for select
+using (created_by = auth.uid());
+
+create policy "resume_shares_insert_own"
+on public.resume_shares for insert
+with check (created_by = auth.uid());
+
+create policy "resume_shares_update_own"
+on public.resume_shares for update
+using (created_by = auth.uid())
